Headline: Queen Elizabeth Ranges
Description: The Queen Elizabeth Ranges is a group of mountain ranges in the Canadian Rockies on the southeastern side of Jasper National Park, Canada. The northern end of the ranges begins east of Medicine Lake and extends in a southeasterly direction past the southern shore of Maligne Lake. The group was named in 1953 to celebrate the coronation of Elizabeth II as Canada's sovereign.[4]

Headline: Lymania corallina
Description: Lymania corallina is a plant species in the genus Lymania. This species is endemic to Brazil.

Headline: Henricus bleptus
Description: Henricus bleptus is a species of moth of the family Tortricidae. It is found in Ecuador (Pichincha Province, Carchi Province).[2]

Headline: Organic décollage
Description: Organic décollage is a phrase used by the photographer Maria Stengard-Green[1] to describe the naturally occurring or non-artistically organized décollage that echoes the work of Mimmo Rotella, Raymond Hains, Jacques Villeglé, Yves Klein and Robert Rauschenberg, and whose antecedents probably influenced their pioneering work.

Headline: Blue Revolution Party
Description: Blue Revolution is a British political party based in Boston, Lincolnshire. It was founded in 2017 by former Conservative Boston borough councillor, Mike Gilbert.[1][2] The party aims to give many people, from various backgrounds, the chance to run in elections, without traditional party structures. It tries to achieve this by giving anyone who endorses the party's core values of "Contract", "Choice" and "Consent" the ability to use its logo, branding and party material.[3]
